news_bbc.title: Most bus fares in England to be capped at £2 from January
news_bbc.summary: The government says the policy will "help with the cost of living and give people the breathing space they need"

news_bbc.title: Will AI help you do your job or replace you?
news_bbc.summary: Artificial Intelligence companies make vast claims about their tools replacing human labour - our charts show what's happening.

news_bbc.title: Lower fuel, chocolate and beef prices help push inflation down
news_bbc.summary: June's fall in inflation will be welcomed by the new prime minister but analysts warn the fall is temporary.

news_bbc.title: 'I can't afford to turn the oven on': 7.4m households struggling to buy essentials
news_bbc.summary: As Andy Burnham promises “breathing space” for households, millions are unable to buy items such as clothing, heating and food.

news_bbc.title: Tokyo urges men to wear shorts to work, but some women say it's 'leg hair harassment'
news_bbc.summary: The Tokyo Metropolitan Government wants workers to ditch the suit and tie for t-shirts, trainers - and shorts.

news_bbc.title: Boost youth jobs by cutting employers' national insurance, MPs urge
news_bbc.summary: A select committee says it has heard "overwhelming evidence" that rising employment costs are reducing training and job vacancies.

news_bbc.title: VAT to be cut from household electricity bills in October
news_bbc.summary: Homes will save around £45 a year through the cut announced on Andy Burnham's first full day as prime minister.

news_bbc.title: France passes law banning under-15s from social media
news_bbc.summary: The law will require social media platforms in France to verify all users age from January.
